patch.value: Harden extension install against path traversal and fill missing health/validate MCP schema props

Pre-existing issues surfaced during PR #69 review and fixed in the same PR: (1) SECURITY: GitHub install subpath (user-controlled, may contain ..) could resolve a manifest.json outside the cloned repo via path.resolve — added an isPathWithinDirectory containment guard in resolveGithubSourceDirectory. (2) SECURITY: normalizeManagedDirectoryName accepted '.' and '..' (they survive the slug regex), letting manifest-controlled input target the extensions root or its parent — now rejected. (3) isPmExecutableToken missed bare Windows 'pm.cmd'/'pm.exe' tokens in linked-test command detection — added. (4) AGENT SCHEMA: PM_TOOL_PARAMETER_PROPERTIES lacked skipVectors/skipIntegrity/skipDrift/verboseDiagnostics although the health/validate action contracts reference them; with additionalProperties:false the generated MCP/SDK schemas rejected those valid options — added the property definitions. Regression tests added (extension-shared-helpers, linked-command-detection, extension-loader non-Error throw).

author: codex
message: Continue PR #69 reviewer fixes; existing local patches cover security/schema/token-detection items.

patch.value: Implemented PR #69 security, schema, linked-test, and handler error-surfacing fixes with focused regression coverage.
Reviewer-identified high-value extension install, schema, linked-test, and handler error cases are blocked or surfaced clearly.

Path traversal candidates are contained, dot/dot-dot managed names are rejected, bare Windows pm executables are detected, health/validate schemas include their diagnostic flags, and non-Error handler messages are surfaced.
